name: Monochrome Kinetic Spatial Gallery Hero
description: A black-void agency landing page structured as a spatial gallery, not a scrolling page — a single word set in a compressed grotesque at 25vw floats centered in a 90vh empty viewport with letter-spacing that animates from 0 to 0.2em as you scroll away. Below the hero void, project names are positioned ABSOLUTELY across a full-viewport scatter field at varying sizes (3vw–7vw) and asymmetric coordinates, not in rows or grids — hovering any name materializes a white floating card (260px) that follows the cursor with details inside. The marquee is NOT a section — it is a barely-visible 28px belt at 12% opacity functioning as background texture. A statement section dims prepositions and articles to 10% opacity so only the nouns and verbs are readable. The only navigational chrome is a single 1px white horizontal rule. The cursor is replaced with a 48px circle using mix-blend-mode difference that grows to 80px on interactive elements. No color. No cards. No grids. The void IS the layout — negative space is 70% of the page area.

vibes.mood: Feels like walking through a gallery where the labels are the art — project names float in the void at different scales like exhibits in a darkened room, the inverted-circle cursor is your flashlight, and hovering anything summons a white card that appears with the quiet confidence of a gallery didactic. The whole page is 70% nothing, and the nothing is the point.

meta.audience: Brands looking for creative agencies that treat their own website as proof of capability — those who believe the portfolio entry starts at the URL bar and that a custom cursor is a design decision, not a gimmick
